data.narrative:
HistoSonics, a Series D biotech company founded by Charles Cain, Brian Fowlkes, Tim Hall, Zhen Xu, and William Roberts, carries a current post-money valuation of $2.25 billion against $250 million in total capital raised. The company operates within a sector comparables universe of 11 private peers and 3 public benchmarks (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L), all in Biotech & Health at Series C through Series E stages.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D advancement, yielding $3.4 billion. Peer-median analysis across five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Formation Bio ($1.7B Series D), Insitro ($2.4B Series C), Ultima Genomics ($4.0B Series C), Abridge ($5.3B Series E), and Colossal Biosciences ($1.5B Series C)—produces a median of $2.4 billion, with a range of $1.5 billion to $5.3 billion. Public-comp anchoring is not applicable; while the Biotech & Health sector median trades at 7.8x price-to-sales across three public tickers, the target's revenue is not disclosed.

The triangulated valuation range is $2.0 billion to $3.9 billion, with a median of $2.9 billion. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion among peer valuations (reflecting stage and business maturity differences) and the absence of revenue-based metrics to calibrate against public comparables.
